symptoms.symptom_name: Vesiculobullous eruption on the exposed dorsal forearms/hands arising ~12 hours after garden brush contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pruritic vesiculobullous eruption on exposed skin arising within the delayed-hypersensitivity window after a clear plant exposure is the defining time-course of plant contact dermatitis.
The hyper-acute single-exposure onset localised to exposed skin argues strongly against a subacute/chronic widespread autoimmune blistering disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Linear/geometric streaking of erythema and blisters tracing the path of plant contact

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Linear/geometric streaks conforming to where plant matter dragged across the skin are near-defining for an external plant contact dermatitis and distinguish it from endogenous or infective eruptions.
Zoster follows a unilateral dermatome, not the external geometry of plant contact across both arms.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus/burning predominant over pain, preventing sleep

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intense pruritus is characteristic of an eczematous/allergic contact process.
Zoster is dominated by neuropathic pain rather than itch; itch predominance argues against it.
Impetigo is generally not intensely pruritic.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral, roughly symmetrical distribution over both forearms/hands (not a single dermatome)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bilateral symmetrical exposure-pattern eruption is incompatible with a unilateral single-dermatome zoster.
Bilateral involvement of both reaching arms fits the mechanics of two-handed brush clearing.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial/lip/tongue/throat swelling, no breathing difficulty, no widespread urticaria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of mucocutaneous swelling, airway symptoms and systemic urticaria makes an evolving systemic allergic reaction unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target.

symptoms.symptom_name: No mucosal (mouth/eye/genital) involvement, no skin detachment, no new oral drug

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Severe mucocutaneous drug reaction (Stevens-Johnson Syndrome / TEN)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of mucosal erosions, skin detachment and a drug trigger argues strongly against SJS/TEN, though the blistering mandates keeping it as a safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile, clear (non-purulent) blister fluid, no spreading heat/induration beyond the affected skin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No systemic or spreading features makes an established secondary cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net target given the ruptured excoriated bullae.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 135/85, HR 90, Temp 36.8, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 72 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; normal, afebrile and haemodynamically stable vitals argue against a systemic reaction. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Prompt in-person or video review of the skin, blisters and mucous membranes to confirm contact dermatitis and exclude SJS/TEN or early secondary infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct inspection of the vesicles/bullae and mucosae, and palpation for early cellulitis, are the assessments telehealth cannot fully do, and review is needed to obtain the oral corticosteroid script.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Later patch testing to identify the culprit plant/contact allergen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Elective allergist/dermatology patch testing after the acute episode settles helps identify the allergen and prevent recurrence; not required acutely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wound swab for bacterial microscopy and culture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated now — no fever, spreading erythema or purulence. Reserve for if secondary bacterial infection develops.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Systemic bloods / septic screen / skin biopsy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No systemic features and a classic contact-dermatitis picture; acute bloods or biopsy would be over-investigation of a localised, clinically-evident condition.
